rag_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure that all identifiers and types are visible within the current AADL scope and match declared property sets or packages.
Place component type contracts in the appropriate annex scope, using only visible features and avoiding implementation-specific subcomponent paths.
generic_rules: Use only visible identifiers and types in AGREE annexes to prevent resolution errors.
Ensure type declarations in AGREE match AADL scalar types (int, real, bool) to avoid resolution failures.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs, avoiding implementation-level dependencies in component type contracts.
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to component ports.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `eq` for helper declarations and `const` for named constants in AGREE.
Ensure that all identifiers used in AGREE expressions are declared or visible in the same annex or AADL scope.
Avoid referencing package-qualified identifiers unless they are explicitly imported via `with` clauses.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce identifiers or types that are not visible in the current AADL scope or declared in the annex.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes; use implementation scopes instead.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
